title: The Mill Where Grain Dreams of Becoming

features.name: millstone-pair
features.desc-on-touch: the stones that forget what they are grinding; between them, the grain learns what it might become.

features.name: flour-ghost-rising
features.desc-on-touch: grain dust hanging in the air like a memory of bread not yet baked; the mill's breath is pure transformation.

features.name: dough-in-moonlight
features.desc-on-touch: risen bread remembering it was grain; the baker's hands are younger here, and the night has just begun.

features.name: water-turning-wheel
features.desc-on-touch: the current that moves the stone; it flows in circles, learning the same lesson twice, forever.

features.name: recipe-written-in-flour
features.desc-on-touch: instructions left by the last baker, dusty now, written on the counter in grain that has become words.

inscription: grain remembers all the shapes it will learn before becoming bread

file.name: BakersHandwritten_Sourdough_Recipe_Undated.txt
file.note: the note in the margin says: 'the mill teaches it, not us'

reverie.title: REVERIE — the mill at dusk where flour becomes light
narr: you stand in the mill as the sun goes down outside the
high window. the stones turn slowly now, the water still
singing. she traces a finger through the flour dust on
the bench, watching it catch gold and purple. 'everything
returns,' she says. 'just in different shapes.' you watch
her hand emerge from the dust as if she were being born.

exits.label: back where the grain stands in the field
upstream where the water learns to turn
down to the bakery where bread remembers the mill
deeper into the dark where grain stores itself
